title: Anti-Trump 'No Kings' Protests Erupt Across Carolinas
summary: Demonstrations against President Trump's administration are taking place in North and South Carolina, focusing on government corruption.
charlotte_impact: Highlights ongoing political divisions and public dissatisfaction with the current administration's policies.

title: Fire Weather Warning Issued for Weekend
summary: A fire weather warning is in effect for Saturday due to cooler, drier conditions, increasing the risk of wildfires.
charlotte_impact: Residents need to be cautious to prevent wildfires, protecting lives and property during dry conditions.

title: Rabies Cases Confirmed in Lincoln County
summary: Two new rabies cases confirmed in Lincoln County from a skunk and a fox. Pet owners urged to update rabies vaccinations.
charlotte_impact: Public health concern; pet owners must vaccinate their animals to prevent the spread of rabies.

title: Fatal Crash on I-85 South Claims One Life
summary: One person is dead following an early morning crash on I-85 South near University City Boulevard, according to Medic.
charlotte_impact: Traffic fatalities highlight the need for safer driving practices and improved road safety measures.

title: Overnight Shooting Injures One in West Charlotte
summary: One person was injured in a shooting near Freedom Drive in west Charlotte around 2 a.m., Medic confirmed.
charlotte_impact: Gun violence continues to be a concern, impacting community safety and requiring law enforcement response.

title: Three Injured in Northeast Charlotte Crash
summary: Three people are hospitalized with serious injuries after a crash in northeast Charlotte early Saturday, according to Medic.
charlotte_impact: Serious traffic accidents raise concerns about road safety and the potential for severe injuries.

title: Woman Fatally Shot at North Charlotte Apartment
summary: A woman was shot and killed through her front door late Thursday at a north Charlotte apartment complex.
charlotte_impact: Tragic incident highlighting the dangers of gun violence and the need for community safety measures.

title: Trump Signs Order to Pay TSA, Easing Airport Delays
summary: President Trump signed an executive order to pay TSA workers, alleviating long airport wait times due to unpaid agents.
charlotte_impact: Ensures smooth airport operations and reduces travel disruptions by compensating essential TSA personnel.

title: Gunfire Strikes East Charlotte Restaurant
summary: Surveillance video captures chaotic moments as gunfire hits an east Charlotte restaurant, frightening patrons.
charlotte_impact: Illustrates the pervasive fear and disruption caused by gun violence in public spaces.

title: Kathie Dello: NC's First Female State Climatologist
summary: A look at Kathie Dello's career as North Carolina's first female state climatologist, serving from 2019 to 2025.
charlotte_impact: Recognizes the achievements of women in science and highlights contributions to environmental understanding.

title: Judge Considers Legality of CMPD's Force During 2020 Protests
summary: A judge is weighing whether CMPD used unlawful force during the 2020 protests, examining police conduct.
charlotte_impact: Addresses concerns about police accountability and the rights of protesters during civil unrest.

title: Uptown Redevelopment Project Announced
summary: A major uptown redevelopment will bring 448 apartments and new retail near Bank of America Stadium.
charlotte_impact: Contributes to urban growth and provides new housing and commercial opportunities in the city center.

title: Shelter Age Limits Leave Families Homeless
summary: Charlotte mom says shelter age limits leave families with teens without options, forcing some to live in cars.
charlotte_impact: Highlights gaps in social services and the challenges faced by homeless families with teenagers.

title: CMPD Officer Charged with Assault
summary: CMPD officer Ryan Clement is on unpaid administrative leave after being charged with assault on a female.
charlotte_impact: Addresses issues of police misconduct and accountability within the Charlotte-Mecklenburg Police Department.

title: South Carolina Implements Statewide Burn Ban
summary: South Carolina Forestry Commission issues a statewide burn ban effective March 27, due to dry conditions.
charlotte_impact: Aims to prevent wildfires and protect natural resources during periods of high fire risk.
